parsed_review.summary: CP00-808 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk B, 40 units RP26.P06.M04.S19-RP26.P06.M05.S28) is a descriptor-only pack. Verified by direct source inspection only (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash/commands executed): (1) createEnterpriseSaasCp808PermissionAuditBindingBridgeDescriptor (packages/enterprise/src/service.js:1466) exists, sources from the CP807 descriptor (source_descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp807PermissionMatrixWorkflowTailDescriptor), and carries the correct authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-809) at service.js:1486-1491. (2) Coverage reconciles exactly with the prompt: deliverable counts test:8/hermes_evidence:2/claude_review:3/implementation:16/security_audit:7/ui:4 = 40 and micro split Secondary Workflow Slice:12 / Permission And Audit Binding:28; row math checks out from registry.js arrays (PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS=20, TEST_ROWS=2, EVIDENCE_HANDOFF_ROWS=8): M04 = slice(18,20)=2 + 2 + 8 = 12; M05 = 20 + 2 + slice(0,6)=6 = 28 (registry.js:1844-1855). (3) validateCoverage (validators.js:126) and validateDescriptor (validators.js:162) perform real, non-vacuous assertions (pack/risk/unit/first/last/duplicate/RP26-only, all distribution maps, per-section row membership, authority flags, and per-row descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / no tenant·SSO·SCIM·secret payloads / no runtime permission·audit·UI·API·identity·cross-tenant·key-material), and model.test.js:863-927 runs them against the real docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (planPack reads the live file; CP00-808 is present at plan line 398 with matching risk/units/range, deviation_from_plan=false) asserting validation.errors==[]. (4) No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries enforced at multiple layers: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:409) and createCaseSet row defaults (service.js:267-279) set all runtime/leak flags false, and validateDescriptor rejects any drift. (5) Hermes used only for a descriptor-only evidence packet (createEnterpriseSaasCp808HermesEvidencePacket service.js:1755: writes_product_state=false, emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write); Claude packet (service.js:2320) is read_only=true, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. (6) Contract/plan/README/index/script are mutually consistent: contract latest_pack=CP00-808 (line 70), latest_projection descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp808... (line 30220), projections.cp808 present, validation.valid=true (line 37331); README #cp00-808 section accurate (line 59); index.js wildcard re-exports (export * from registry/service/validators) resolve the validate-script named imports of the CP808 symbols. The manifest makes no premature claim: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, status=in_progress, pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending, valid_review_receipts=[]/invalid_review_attempts=[] — fully consistent with the prompt authority boundary (enterprise trust closed, human approval required, Hermes not embedded in product runtime, Claude read-only and non-final). Unrelated dirty files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, workbook/*, the .docx, the unrelated LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt result) are correctly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and excluded from active pack scope. Review readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON, non-zero stdout (1432 bytes), exit 0; no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/tool-call-shaped output and no prior invalid CP808 review evidence exists. As read-only independent reviewer (not final approver) I find no P0/P1/P2 issues; the bounded descriptor-verified completion claim is supported. One P3 documents that downstream closeout artifacts and the validation-command run are not yet present/executed — expected at this review stage and outside what a read-only review can confirm.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts and validation-command evidence not yet present (expected at the Claude-review stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and every included unit's evidence_refs point to artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-808/review-receipt.json plus those four files, but only manifest.json currently exists under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-808/ (Glob confirms) and the artifacts dir has no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json yet. pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending with valid_review_receipts=[]. This is the expected state during the Claude review step: those artifacts, plus execution of the listed validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check), are produced/run by the closeout pipeline after this review. Being read-only, I verified the descriptor (service.js:1466), registry binding/requirements (registry.js:392,1820), no-write attestation (registry.js:409), validators (validators.js:126,162,671,675,689), tests (model.test.js:863-927), contract promotion/projection/validation.valid, live plan entry (plan line 398), README, and index re-exports by source inspection only; I did not and cannot execute commands or confirm downstream adjudication/inspection. No defect in the pack's current claims was found - the manifest correctly reports production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / in_progress and does not overclaim.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the normal closeout pipeline: run the listed validation commands, capture command-evidence.json, and generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json; then obtain human final approval before any runtime or enterprise-trust opening (deferred to CP00-809 / RP26.P06.M05.S29). No code change required for this pack.
